mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 2.5 million signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with high initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with high housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 104 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases production costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with low industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals a monetary policy response to inflation that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows and boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 2.5 million and a 2-month decline in industrial production below 104 signals a weakening labor market and supply chain that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with high initial unemployment claims and low industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million and a 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2.5% signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate and consumer spending that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with high housing starts and high real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 104 and a 2-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals a weakening supply chain and labor market that reduces corporate profitability and increases production costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with low industrial production and high unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
